perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this MNDA without cause upon five (5) days written notice given to the other, provided that confidentiality obligations under Section A of this Attachment A shall survive termination hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this MNDA without cause upon five (5) days written notice given to the other; however, termination shall not affect any obligations regarding confidentiality, which remain in effect indefinitely regardless of termination.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that 'confidentiality obligations remain in effect indefinitely regardless of termination,' we contradict the original condition that confidentiality survives termination only under Section A of Attachment A. This creates confusion about whether other parts of the MNDA also survive termination, creating an in-text contradiction about the effects of termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this MNDA without cause upon five (5) days written notice given to the other; however, termination shall not affect any obligations regarding confidentiality, which remain in effect indefinitely regardless of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive termination only under Section A of Attachment A. The changed text broadens this, stating that confidentiality obligations remain in effect indefinitely regardless of termination. This contradicts the original limitation and creates uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality obligations post-termination, as it is no longer clear if Section A of Attachment A is the only section that survives termination. This is a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction because the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. The Change Text contradicts the original text's limitation by removing the restriction on Section A of Attachment A.

perturbation.original_text: This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except by the Vice President of Marketing, irrespective of written agreements between parties.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction regarding how the MNDA can be modified. Originally, modifications required a signed writing by both parties. The altered clause states that the Vice President of Marketing can change the agreement regardless of the original written agreement. This creates uncertainty about the legitimate modification process and contradicts the previous clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except by the Vice President of Marketing, irrespective of written agreements between parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement clause about MNDA modifications. The original required both parties to sign any changes, while the altered text gives the Vice President of Marketing the sole power to make modifications, irrespective of written agreements. This directly contradicts the principle of mutual agreement and introduces uncertainty, which is a strong in-text contradiction. Since the Contradicted Text section is the same as the Changed Text section, the change directly contradicts the original agreement. Based on the Perturbation Explanation given, this is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Consistent with other provisions herein, each party assures that it will not knowingly, without obtaining prior authorization from the U.S. Dept. of Commerce Office of Export Administration, transmit directly or indirectly the technical data received pursuant hereto or the immediate product (including processes and services) produced directly by use of such technical data to Afghanistan, People's Republic of China, or any other Country Group Q, S, W, T, or Z country specified in Supplement No. I to Part 370 of U.S. Dept. of Commerce Export Administration Regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding other provisions herein, each party is permitted to transmit technical data received pursuant hereto or the immediate product (including processes and services) produced directly by use of such technical data to Afghanistan, People's Republic of China, or any other Country Group Q, S, W, T, or Z country specified in Supplement No. I to Part 370 of U.S. Dept. of Commerce Export Administration Regulations.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Notwithstanding other provisions herein' and the removal of the restriction on transmitting technical data to specific countries directly contradicts the original clause's explicit export control restrictions. This creates a direct conflict about whether the parties are allowed to share data with the specified countries, causing uncertainty and undermining the original intent of the export control provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding other provisions herein, each party is permitted to transmit technical data received pursuant hereto or the immediate product (including processes and services) produced directly by use of such technical data to Afghanistan, People's Republic of China, or any other Country Group Q, S, W, T, or Z country specified in Supplement No. I to Part 370 of U.S. Dept. of Commerce Export Administration Regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits transmitting technical data to specific countries without prior authorization. The changed text, through the addition of 'Notwithstanding other provisions herein' and the removal of the restriction, now permits the transmission of this data, which is also mentioned as the contradicted text. This directly contradicts the original restriction, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text allows something the original text forbids, creating uncertainty regarding export control obligations.

perturbation.original_text: No rights or obligations other than expressly recited herein are to be implied here from. Nothing except that expressly stated herein shall affect either party's present or prospective rights under any country's patent laws, or be construed as granting any license under any present or future patent or application therefor, or preclude marketing any product unless such marketing constitutes unauthorized disclosure of INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: Certain implied rights and obligations, not expressly recited herein, may arise from the nature of this agreement. Nothing except that expressly stated herein shall affect either party's present or prospective rights under any country's patent laws, or be construed as granting any license under any present or future patent or application therefor, or preclude marketing any product unless such marketing constitutes unauthorized disclosure of INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing the possibility of 'implied rights and obligations' contradicts the original statement that ONLY expressly recited rights and obligations apply. This adds ambiguity and conflicts with the intention of the clause to limit interpretations to the explicit terms, undermining the predictability of contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Certain implied rights and obligations, not expressly recited herein, may arise from the nature of this agreement. Nothing except that expressly stated herein shall affect either party's present or prospective rights under any country's patent laws, or be construed as granting any license under any present or future patent or application therefor, or preclude marketing any product unless such marketing constitutes unauthorized disclosure of INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies any implied rights or obligations, limiting the agreement to expressly stated terms. The changed text introduces the possibility of 'implied rights and obligations,' directly contradicting the original clause's intent to exclude any non-explicit obligations. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text and contradicted text are identical, which would cause confusion and undermine the clarity of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This document and appendices, along with any contemporaneous oral agreements, contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By including 'any contemporaneous oral agreements' as part of the entire agreement, it contradicts the initial clause stating that the document contains the *entire* agreement. The original clause supersedes any prior agreements, but with this addition, it is not the case that everything that happened before is not relevant. This addition opens the door to disputes about unwritten terms, conflicting with the original intent of a fully integrated agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices, along with any contemporaneous oral agreements, contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly includes 'any contemporaneous oral agreements' as part of the entire agreement. This directly contradicts the principle that the written document and appendices should represent the complete agreement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, but the original intent, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, was for a fully integrated agreement that supersedes all prior understandings, written or oral. By including contemporaneous oral agreements, it introduces ambiguity and potential disputes about unwritten terms, conflicting with the idea of a clear, self-contained agreement, thus representing a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Structural Flaws definition, as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
